summary: Research latest inference optimizations to beat MNN on Xclipse; design fusion engine

ranking.angle: Quantization / low-precision (int8 W8A8, int4 weight-only) u
Xclipse 960 / AMD RDNA + Samsung SPAL Vulkan-compute specifi
Angle #4 — what makes MNN fast that vxrt has NOT replicated.
Automatic operator fusion to keep intermediates on-chip and

plan.headline: Build an automatic vertical-fusion pass whose FIRST and lowest-risk product is the MobileNetV3 Squeeze-Excite tail collapse (GlobalAvgPool→FC→FC→hardsigmoid→channel-Mul → ONE kernel), then extend the SAME pass to fuse depthwise-3x3 → pointwise-1x1-project (DWPW, zero-recompute) into one kernel that keeps the expanded intermediate on-chip. Rationale: every banked win on this driver came from cutting traffic/dispatches WITHOUT adding occupancy pressure (split-K, precise barriers, Add+Relu epilogue); fusion is the next instance of exactly that, and it is the only lever (besides int8) that cuts the inter-OP activation traffic the prior kernel work never touched. Do NOT lead with int8 or Winograd — both are gated on unverified driver behavior and prior regressions.
plan.rankedPlan:
Ordered by impact x feasibility on this memory-bound SPAL/RDNA GPU. Each step is independently shippable behind a VXRT_* env gate with the existing cosine>=0.999 vs-ORT validation, kept only if it measures a win (same discipline that killed image backend / Winograd / c8w4).

1. SE-TAIL FUSION (kFusedSE), effort S, risk LOW, ship FIRST. Pattern: GlobalAvgPool([N,C,H,W]->[N,C,1,1]) -> Conv1x1/Gemm(reduce) -> Relu -> Conv1x1/Gemm(expand) -> HardSigmoid -> Mul(channel-broadcast against the block input). MobileNetV3-Large has ~11 SE blocks, each currently ~5 separate dispatches over tiny [N,C,1,1] tensors = pure dispatch overhead on a graph already losing ~7ms to inter-dispatch drain. Collapse to 1-2 dispatches (one workgroup does pool-reduce via subgroupAdd over the 64-wide wave, both FCs from registers, hardsigmoid, then the broadcast-Mul folded into the consuming conv's input read or a single scale kernel). NO LDS reduction loop over large tensors, NO occupancy risk. Expected: MobileNetV3 ~5-10% wall (removes ~33-44 dispatches). This alone narrows the 15% MNV3 gap meaningfully and de-risks the fusion-pass infrastructure.

2. DWPW FUSION (kFusedDwPw): depthwise-3x3 (+act) -> pointwise-1x1-project(group1,s1,p0) into ONE kernel, expanded intermediate staged on-chip. Effort M, risk MEDIUM (LDS). Targets MobileNetV2 (~10 inverted residuals) and MobileNetV3 (the non-SE-blocked pairs). Removes the write+read round-trip of the EXPANDED tensor (the block's largest activation) AND one dispatch+RAW barrier per pair. Expected: MobileNetV2 +10-18% (tie -> clear win), MobileNetV3 +8-15% (combined with step 1, flips it ahead). CRITICAL: gate per-shape (only fuse when (a) DW output has exactly one consumer = the project PW, i.e. NOT an SE-branched DW, and (b) expanded-intermediate bytes exceed an L2 threshold AND it is NOT a split-K-eligible deep/low-parallelism shape — split-K is the banked win and is mutually exclusive with this fusion). So DWPW self-selects to the larger-spatial early/mid blocks; deep 7x7 blocks keep split-K.

3. int8 W8A8 PHASE-1 weight-only on deep 1x1 (activations stay fp16, dequant-in-shader), effort M, risk LOW accuracy. ONLY after a standalone microbench (256->256@14x14 and a large-spatial 3x3, mirroring vx_image_bench) confirms dotPacked4x8AccSatEXT is truly accelerated and a conversion-inclusive conv beats fp16 on SPAL. RDNA-pre-4 gives int8 the SAME per-clock throughput as fp16, so the win is bandwidth-only; do not port without the number. Expected: ResNet/MobileNet deep-1x1 weight traffic halved -> ~3-7%.

4. int8 W8A8 PHASE-2 full activation+weight int8 on ResNet 3x3 group-1 convs (the 43ms core), per-output-channel weight scale + per-tensor (or per-channel-input if cosine drops) activation scale, quant/dequant FUSED into producer/consumer epilogues (never standalone dispatches). Effort L, risk MEDIUM. This is the ONLY lever that touches ResNet's gap (ResNet has no depthwise, so fusion steps 1-2 do nothing for it). Expected ResNet 1.2-1.5x on the 3x3 core (NOT the oversold 4.1x/1.8x) -> ~52.8ms toward ~40-44ms, enough to reach/beat MNN (46.4) if conversion cost is fused away.

5. (Parallel cheap probe, effort S) Multi-queue async-compute for ResNet's ~7ms inter-dispatch drain. Device has VK_KHR_timeline_semaphore but currently ONE compute queue (queueCount=1) — needs backend change to create a 2nd queue and split independent bottleneck-block stages across queues with semaphores instead of barriers. Try as a cheap signal; benefit on SPAL unverified.

Inception already wins (+20%) — leave it fp16, ensure new passes don't fuse across the parallel-branch boundaries that precise barriers currently overlap.
plan.firstKernel:
Ship step 1 (SE-tail) first because it has zero occupancy risk and proves the fusion-pass plumbing. But the load-bearing NEW conv kernel is the DWPW fused shader (shaders/fused_dwpw_fp16.comp). Exact sketch:

THREAD/WORKGROUP MAPPING: local_size_x=64 (wave64). One workgroup owns one output spatial TILE of T pixels (start T=4) x one PROJECT-PW output channel-block (4 oc). Total dispatch groups = N * projCoutb * ceil(OHW/T).

WHAT STAYS ON-CHIP: the depthwise output for the tile, across ALL expanded channel-blocks, in an LDS commBuffer. THIS IS THE KEY CORRECTION to the register-only proposals: a 1x1 PW reduces over every expanded channel per output pixel, so output pixel (x,y) needs all Cinb_exp DW results at (x,y). A pure-register design would force the DW to be recomputed projCoutb times (re-reading DW input 4-40x) — that is the trap. Stage the DW tile in LDS so the whole workgroup's PW phase reads it once. To dodge the documented LDS regressions: lay commBuffer out SCALAR/SoA as `shared float comm[Cexp_blocks][T*4]` (NOT f16vec4 — RDNA float4-LDS = 8 bank conflicts vs 2 for scalar), cap it <=16KB (64KB LDS / 4 to keep >=4 waves/CU), and if Cexp*T exceeds the cap, fall back to unfused.

PHASE A (DW): the 64 threads cooperatively compute depthwise-3x3 (stride s, pad 1) over the input halo for the tile, for every expanded channel-block; write results into comm[]; apply the DW's fused act here. barrier().
PHASE B (PW): each thread takes its 4 project-oc x T pixels, loops icb over comm[] doing dot-products into 4 fp32 vec4 accumulators (reuse the existing conv1x1_fp16 microkernel body verbatim — acc.x += dot(inv,w0) ...); epilogue +bias, +residual (HAS_RES spec const, reuse), fused act; write project output once.

WEIGHTS: keep BOTH weight sets in global SSBO (they already L2-cache per measured facts — do NOT preload to LDS, that is the pressure that hurt). DW weights packed [Cb][KH][KW][4] (existing layout), PW weights [Cout][Cinb][4] (existing conv1x1 layout). Both via uploadCached.

INTEGRATION in conv.cpp: add a `fusedDwPw` branch in ConvOp::prepare()/record() analogous to the existing depthwise/pointwise/splitk branches. Detect via a new Node flag (fusedProjectWeightId carrying the PW weights/bias/act/residual onto the DW node). prepare() builds the fused pipeline, uploads both weight sets, computes commBuffer size and the per-shape eligibility (T, Cexp, LDS cap, NOT-split-K-eligible). record() emits ONE dispatch of ldsGroups. Reuse pickLocalSize scratch-buffer autotune extended to a small (T) candidate set {4,8}. Gate behind VXRT_FUSE_DWPW=1.

Build order within the kernel: get correctness first with a naive register-spill version vs CPU oracle (cosine>=0.999), then add the scalar-SoA LDS commBuffer, then autotune T, then A/B vs the unfused baseline per shape and keep only winning shapes.
plan.fusionEngineDesign:
Automatic operator-merge pass, modeled exactly on the existing fuseResidualAdd/fuseActivations in src/import/passes.cpp (build a producer[] map, pattern-match, check consumer-count==1, rewire consumers, mark a fusion flag on the surviving node, remove the absorbed nodes). v1 ships as a small set of hand-written fused KERNELS selected by hand-written pattern MATCHERS in the pass; the design generalizes to a table-driven matcher later.

ARCHITECTURE (3 layers):
1. PATTERN MATCHER (passes.cpp): a list of FusionPattern entries, each = {ordered op-type sequence, predicate(nodes) for legality, emit(graph, matched_nodes) that produces the fused node}. Walk g.nodes; for each candidate producer try each pattern's linear chain following the single-consumer edges (require every interior tensor has exactly one consumer and is not a graph output — same check already used). On match, call emit and mark absorbed nodes for removal. This is DNNFusion's idea (classify ops, fuse producer-consumer chains) reduced to a few concrete patterns.
   - Pattern SE: GlobalAvgPool -> Conv1x1/Gemm -> Relu -> Conv1x1/Gemm -> HardSigmoid(Unary) -> Mul(Binary, channel-broadcast). emit -> kFusedSE node carrying both FC weights + the broadcast-Mul's other operand id.
   - Pattern DWPW: Conv(depthwise, any fusedAct) -> Conv(1x1,group1,s1,p0). Legality: DW output single-consumer == the PW; PW not already residual-fused-incompatible. emit -> tag DW node with fusedProjectWeightId etc (carry PW weights/bias). Run BEFORE fuseResidualAdd so a trailing residual still folds into the project epilogue (the existing HAS_RES path composes).
2. COST/ELIGIBILITY GUARD (FusePlanner-lite, per the FCM cost model): emit() only commits the fusion if estimated fused global-memory-access < layer-by-layer AND #output-tiles >= a GPU-fill threshold AND commBuffer fits the LDS cap AND the shape is not split-K-eligible. Encodes FCM's "fuse iff GMA(fused)<GMA(LBL) and #tiles>=#SMs". For shapes that fail, leave the graph unfused (fall back to the proven separate kernels). This is what prevents the regression-on-small-spatial trap.
3. KERNEL SELECTION (conv.cpp ops): the fused node's op picks the fused pipeline; if a runtime check (LDS size, autotune) says unfused is faster, it can still dispatch the two original kernels (keep the original weights resident) — i.e. fusion is a per-shape SELECTION like MNN's create()-time chooser, never an unconditional rewrite.

Run order in runStandardPasses: inferShapes; eliminateIdentity; foldBatchNorm; fuseActivations; fuseSE; fuseDwPw; fuseResidualAdd; constFold; eliminateDeadNodes; inferShapes. Each new pass logs count fused, same as existing passes.

GENERALIZATION PATH (v2): replace the hand-written matcher list with a small classifier (op -> {one-to-one elementwise, reduction, conv-heavy}) and the DNNFusion legality rule (fuse a one-to-one/elementwise/reduction chain into the nearest heavy op's epilogue; fuse heavy+heavy only when BOTH are memory-bound per the ShaderNN/FCM caveat). The epilogue-fusion half already exists (act+residual); v2 just makes it data-driven. Keep codegen as a few parameterized templates, not a full compiler.
plan.expectedResult:
Honest assessment: YES on MobileNetV3, MobileNetV2, SqueezeNet via fusion; ResNet-50 requires the int8 track and is the riskiest. By model:

- MobileNetV3-Large (now ~15% behind, 22.5 vs 21.6/19.6): SE-tail collapse (step 1, ~5-10%) + DWPW fusion on non-SE pairs (step 2, ~8-15%) -> realistically ~18-20ms. Beats MNN avg (21.6), roughly ties MNN min (19.6). LIKELY WIN vs avg, plausible tie/edge vs min.
- MobileNetV2 (~tie, 15.4 vs 14.0/10.3): DWPW fusion on ~10 inverted residuals -> ~13-14ms. Beats MNN avg, approaches but may NOT beat MNN's cold min (10.3) — that gap is partly thermal, not kernel.
- SqueezeNet (~tie): fire modules are 1x1+(1x1||3x3), little depthwise -> small ~3-6% from any 1x1 epilogue fusion; stays at/just above tie. Honest: marginal.
- Inception-v3: already +20%, untouched.
- ResNet-50 (~15% behind, 52.8 vs 46.4/43.5): fusion does NOTHING (no depthwise). Needs int8 W8A8 on the 3x3 core. Realistic 1.2-1.5x on the 43ms conv core (NOT the headline 4.1x — that was Adreno with proven DP4a; RDNA-pre-4 int8 is bandwidth-only) -> ~40-46ms. Beats MNN ONLY if (a) the microbench confirms accelerated int8 and (b) quant/dequant is fully fused into epilogues. This is the one model where "beat MNN on EVERY model" is genuinely at risk.

Magnitude of beat: where it wins (MNV2/MNV3/SqueezeNet) expect 5-20% ahead of MNN avg, slimmer vs MNN cold-min. ResNet best case ~5-10% ahead, realistic case ~tie, worst case still behind if int8 doesn't accelerate. Direction is high-confidence (bytes saved are real and engine is bandwidth-bound); magnitude is moderate-confidence and per-shape measured.
plan.risks:
1. LDS OCCUPANCY CLIFF (DWPW step 2): the commBuffer + barrier is the SAME mechanism that regressed conv3x3_lds (56ms) and shared-mem-1x1 here. Mitigation: scalar/SoA layout (2 vs 8 bank conflicts), <=16KB cap, weights stay in global, gate per-shape behind a measured-win check, default-off until it beats baseline. This is unproven on SPAL — treat step 2 as experiment, build the SE-tail (step 1) and a register-resident smallest-block prototype first.

2. FUSION/SPLIT-K MUTUAL EXCLUSION: DWPW fusion cannot apply to deep low-parallelism blocks because split-K (the biggest banked win, MNV2 14.7->10.1) needs them spread across thread-groups. So fusion self-selects to larger-spatial blocks where the round-trip is a smaller traffic share — the eligibility guard MUST enforce this or it will regress the deep blocks.

3. int8 DRIVER EMULATION (steps 3-4): SPAL may advertise "8-bit accelerated" yet lower dotPacked4x8AccSatEXT to multiply-extend-add, and RDNA-pre-4 has no int8 compute boost anyway. Gate the entire int8 effort on a standalone microbench BEFORE porting (same discipline that killed the image backend). If it doesn't beat fp16 conversion-inclusive, int8 is dead and ResNet needs the production fused-cooperative Winograd instead (the substantial kernel BENCHMARK.md already flags).

4. QUANT/DEQUANT DISPATCH TAX: mixed-precision boundaries add dispatches on a graph already bleeding ~7ms to drain; the win is cancelled unless quant fuses into the producer epilogue and dequant into the consumer (the engine already fuses Add+Relu, so the pattern exists).

5. ACCURACY: per-tensor activation int8 can breach cosine>=0.999 on outlier-heavy MNV3 SE/hardswish layers; fix needs per-channel-input or a dynamic max-reduce pass that costs a dispatch (erodes the win). Keep depthwise + first conv fp16.

6. ResNet is the only model where the goal may not be met by the highest-confidence lever (fusion); it depends entirely on the lower-confidence int8/Winograd track. Set expectations accordingly.

7. async multi-queue (step 5) requires a backend change (currently queueCount=1) and SPAL multi-queue overlap is unverified — cheap to try, do not bank on it.

8. Don't let the new passes fuse across Inception's parallel-branch boundaries that precise barriers currently overlap (would regress the one clear win).
